recommendations.title: Adopt border-first elevation with whisper shadows; ban heavy drop shadows and accent-tinted shadows
recommendations.detail: Set card = 1px solid var(--border-subtle) + box-shadow 0 1px 2px rgba(15,15,15,0.04). Popover/menu = +0 4px 12px rgba(15,15,15,0.08). Dragging = 0 8px 24px rgba(15,15,15,0.12). Radius tokens 6/8/12px. This matches Notion's documented near-imperceptible multi-layer model and Linear's 'softened borders over harsh dividers' principle, keeping the dense board calm. Never tint shadows with the indigo accent.
recommendations.where: Component-level card/menu/modal styles; radius + shadow tokens in tokens.css.

recommendations.title: Reserve exactly one indigo accent for interaction state only, never for status
recommendations.detail: --accent #5B5BD6 (oklch 54% 0.20 274) for primary button / active nav / link; --accent-fg oklch(50% 0.19 274) for links on white (AA-safe); --accent-subtle #EEEEFB for selected-row tint; 2px focus ring oklch(62% 0.18 274) with 2px offset. Document the rule that status hues and the accent hue never overlap in meaning — status = data, accent = action. If Agent-Executing's indigo collides with the brand accent, shift that state to violet/285 and desaturate.
recommendations.where: tokens.css accent block; button/link/nav/focus styles. Pairs with the 'restrained accent' brief requirement.

recommendations.title: Encode every status with color + icon-shape + text + data-state attribute (no color-alone)
recommendations.detail: For each of the 12 states render: a unique status glyph (dot/half-circle/check/slash/triangle vocabulary à la Linear), the text label, the tint, AND a machine-readable data-state="<slug>" + aria-label on the element. This satisfies WCAG 1.4.1, survives colorblindness, and makes the board scrapable/diff-able by the autonomous loop (the.kiro evidence/observability requirement). Same pattern for the 22 labels: tint pill + text, never tint-only.
recommendations.where: Board card / status-pill component markup; aligns with webhook_handler.py / the_loop.py state vocabulary so DOM state matches API state.

recommendations.title: Set Linear-grade density: 8px grid (4px half-steps), 13px workhorse body, 11px tracked-uppercase state pills, weight-based hierarchy
recommendations.detail: Column gutter 8px, card padding 8-12px, 4px meta-row rhythm, list-row height 32-36px. Type: 11px/16 state labels (weight 500, +0.04em, uppercase), 13px/18 card title (weight 500), 14px section header, 16px page title. Prefer weight contrast (450 vs 600) over large size jumps to keep it calm. Mirror Linear's refresh: smaller icons, compact pills, muted inactive/secondary text (drop secondary text to L~50%, inactive nav to L~58%).
recommendations.where: Spacing + typography token scales in tokens.css; board layout grid and list/board view components.

recommendations.title: Gate the whole color matrix with APCA (Lc) thresholds, not WCAG 2.x alone, and CI-check it
recommendations.detail: Validate: body/labels Lc>=75, headings Lc>=60, disabled Lc~45; never let secondary text on white go lighter than oklch L~52%. Add a build-time contrast check (e.g. apca-w3 / Culori) over the token pairs so any future hue tweak that breaks 4.5:1 fails CI. Because L is held constant across hues, proving one tint/text pair proves the matrix — but assert it programmatically so machine-readability/observability is enforced, not assumed.
recommendations.where: A token-lint / contrast-check step in the board UI build pipeline (new); guards tokens.css. Complements the existing completeness-gate discipline in the_loop.py.

area: PM-SDLC Board Observability & Legibility — Linear/Notion light-theme restyle (Plane board)
summary: The current board is stock Plane in its default light theme (Inter Variable, body bg oklch(0.9543 0.001 230.67) ≈ #f1f1f3, near-black text oklch(0.2378…) ≈ #2c2e31, an "editor-colors" pastel palette, 0px body radius). Screens 01-03 show a list view, a 12-state kanban (Backlog → Agent-Triaged → Spec-Compiling → Spec-Verified → … → Done + HANDOFF/Blocked/Failed), and an EARS-structured ticket detail with an evidence record. It is functional but generic: status is carried almost entirely by color and tiny dots, column identity is undifferentiated, cards are text-dense without a scan-anchor hierarchy, evidence/traceability/blockers are buried in body prose rather than surfaced as first-class chips, and nothing is exposed for machine parsing beyond Plane's default DOM. The goal — maximal human AND machine legibility in a Linear-crisp / Notion-refined light theme — is reachable through five levers: a calmer neutral light theme (css-theme), a disciplined status/evidence/blocker visual language that never relies on color alone (css-theme + content-convention), Plane workflow/field/label configuration that makes state, ownership, evidence and traceability structurally first-class (workflow-config), card/ticket content conventions that put a scannable anchor row on every item (content-convention), and a machine-readability layer (data-* attributes, ARIA roles, a parallel JSON/JSON-LD projection) so the same board is parseable by agents (custom-property). Concrete token values, contrast ratios and spacing are specified below, drawn from Linear's 2026 UI refresh (calmer, dimmer chrome, redrawn/resized icons, content-forward contrast), Notion's light palette (warm neutrals, #097fe8 action blue, near-imperceptible layered shadows, 8/12px radii), and WCAG 2.2 (4.5:1 text, 3:1 non-text/UI, never color-only).
key_findings: CURRENT STATE: design-tokens.json confirms stock Plane light theme — body bg oklch(0.9543 0.001 230.67) (≈#f1f1f3, a flat cool gray with no surface layering), text oklch(0.2378 0.0029 230.83) (≈#2c2e31), Inter Variable 16px/400, body radius 0px, shadow none. The only chromatic system present is the '--editor-colors-*' pastel pairs (e.g. green-text #0fc27b on green-bg #c3f0de, peach-text #ff5b59 on peach-bg #ffd5d7). There is no dedicated surface/elevated/border token scale, no semantic status palette, and no accent discipline — the opposite of the rationed-accent Linear model.

SCANNABILITY GAP (human): On the kanban (screen 02) every column header looks identical (same weight, same gray, a tiny colored dot + count) so the 12 states do not form a readable left-to-right pipeline; cards are a stack of equal-weight lines (ID, title, a label chip, an avatar) with no dominant scan anchor, so the eye has nothing to fix on. Linear's 2026 refresh solved exactly this by dimming chrome so content stands out, redrawing/resizing icons, and making headers/view-controls consistent — the board needs the same content-forward contrast move.

STATUS RELIES ON COLOR ALONE (accessibility + machine defect): States and labels are distinguished by hue (the editor-colors dots/chips). WCAG 2.2 SC 1.4.1 forbids color as the only channel; ~8% of users and every colorblind or grayscale viewer loses the pipeline, and a screen-scraping agent gets no reliable signal. Each state needs a SHAPE + ICON + TEXT triad (Linear's pattern: open circle, partial-fill arc, checkmark, etc.), not just a colored dot.

EVIDENCE / TRACEABILITY / BLOCKERS ARE BURIED: The ticket detail (screen 03) carries the 4-field Evidence_Record, EARS clauses, and acceptance criteria as body prose. For a Spec-to-Evidence engine whose entire value proposition is provable traceability, evidence status (proven/unproven), the spec→test→output trace, and blocker reason should be FIRST-CLASS, glanceable chips on the card face and a fixed detail rail — not paragraphs a human must read or an agent must NLP-parse.

NO MACHINE-READABILITY LAYER: Beyond Plane's default React DOM there are no data-* hooks (data-state, data-evidence, data-blocked, data-owner-role, data-trace-id), no explicit ARIA roles on cards/columns (role=listitem/list, aria-label, aria-disabled for terminal states), and no parallel structured projection (JSON / JSON-LD). Per 2025 guidance, ARIA + data attributes act as a 'semantic contract' that lets agents read board state deterministically instead of guessing from rendered text. This is the single highest-leverage transparency gain for the 'parseable by machines' goal.

LINEAR DESIGN MODEL (target): Inter Variable at ~510/590 weights with slight negative tracking; a tight cool-gray monochrome scale with the accent (Linear lavender-blue #5e6ad2) rationed to ONE primary action per screen; cards earn presence from a 1px inset border + a near-imperceptible shadow rather than fills; the whole surface stack stays within a narrow 4-step range from canvas to elevated; dense 'instrument-panel' rhythm where every pixel earns its place.

NOTION REFINED-LIGHT MODEL (target): warm-neutral light palette — page bg #FFFFFF, hover/selected gray #F1F1EF, default text #373530 (warm near-black, softer than pure #000), secondary text #787774; functional blue #097fe8 (CTA) / #0075de (links); 8–12px radius on cards/containers; multi-layer ultra-low-opacity shadows (e.g. rgba(25,25,25,0.027)) for a soft lift not a hard edge; a documented 4/8/12/16/24/32/48/64 spacing scale. NotionInter (Inter superfamily) spans 14–54px for UI.

CONTRAST/WCAG TARGETS: Body and labels need ≥4.5:1; large/heading text and bold ≥3:1; all status icons, borders, focus rings and chip outlines are non-text UI and need ≥3:1 against their background (AA SC 1.4.11). The current near-black-on-#f1f1f3 body text passes (~13:1) but the pastel editor-colors chips (e.g. #0fc27b green-text on #c3f0de bg ≈ 1.9:1) FAIL badly as text and as standalone status indicators — they read as decoration, not data. Pair every chip with a ≥3:1 outline/icon and a text label.

recommendations.title: Replace Plane's flat gray with a 4-step warm-neutral surface stack + rationed single accent
recommendations.detail: Define a token scale (theme override / CSS vars) instead of the single flat oklch(0.9543) body bg. Light theme: --bg-canvas #FBFBFA (Notion-warm app canvas), --bg-surface #FFFFFF (cards/columns), --bg-elevated #FFFFFF + shadow (popovers/detail rail), --bg-hover #F1F1EF, --bg-selected #EAEEF6. Text: --text-primary #2A2A28 (warm near-black, ~14:1 on surface), --text-secondary #6B6B68 (~5.4:1), --text-tertiary #9A9A97 (~3.1:1, metadata only). Borders: --border-subtle #EDEDEC, --border-default #E3E3E0 (both ≥3:1 vs surface for UI-element contrast), --border-strong #D4D4D0. ONE accent, rationed to the primary action/active state only: --accent #5E6AD2 (Linear lavender-blue) or Notion #097fe8 — pick one and use it for ≤1 primary action per view. Keep the surface stack within a narrow luminance range (Linear principle) so elevation reads from border+shadow, not fill.
recommendations.where: audit/plane-ui/design-tokens.json captures the current values to override; apply via a Plane theme/CSS custom-property layer (the :root color vars enumerated there).

recommendations.title: Card & column geometry: tighten to Linear density with Notion softness
recommendations.detail: Cards: padding 10px 12px; gap-between-cards 6px; border-radius 8px; 1px solid var(--border-subtle) inset border + shadow 0 1px 2px rgba(15,15,15,0.04), 0 1px 1px rgba(15,15,15,0.03) (near-imperceptible layered lift, not a hard drop). Column: width 300–320px; header height 40px; column gutter 12px; internal padding 8px. Type ramp (Inter Variable): card title 13px/18px weight 510, letter-spacing -0.05px; metadata 12px/16px weight 450 in --text-secondary; IDs/mono 12px tabular-nums. Section/column header 12px weight 590 uppercase-tracking +0.3px in --text-secondary. Radii: 6px chips, 8px cards, 10px panels (Notion 8–12 band). Spacing scale: 4/8/12/16/24/32. This yields the dense, calm 'instrument-panel' rhythm where the title is the clear scan anchor.
recommendations.where: Plane card/board component CSS (kanban column + issue-card); mirror the home.html token conventions in docs/website/autonomous-agent.dev/mockups/home.html for consistency across the platform.

recommendations.title: Status visual language: never color-alone — shape + icon + text triad for all 12 states
recommendations.detail: Give every workflow state a distinct ICON+SHAPE so the pipeline reads in grayscale and is parseable: Backlog ▷ dashed circle; Agent-Triaged ▷ dotted circle; Spec-Compiling/Executing/In-Verification ▷ partial-fill progress arc (25/50/75%) like Linear's in-progress glyph; Spec-Verified/Plan-Approved ▷ open check; Human-Review ▷ eye/person glyph; Done ▷ filled check (single success green #1A7F4B, ≥4.5:1); HANDOFF ▷ outline diamond (neutral, NOT red — it's terminal-success-adjacent); Blocked ▷ filled octagon (amber #B25E09 outline ≥3:1); Failed ▷ filled triangle (red #C4554D). Each state chip = icon + text label + ≥3:1 outline; reserve saturated fills for Done/Blocked/Failed only, keep the 9 process states in calm neutrals so exceptions pop. Column headers get the same icon at 16px + count + a 2px top accent bar in the state hue so the 12-column pipeline is legible at a glance.
recommendations.where: Plane workflow-state icon set + column-header component; the 12 states are enumerated in plane-integration/README.md §Control vocabulary and docs/plane/PLANE_BLUEPRINT.md §1.

recommendations.title: Surface evidence, traceability and blockers as first-class card chips + a fixed detail rail
recommendations.detail: On the CARD FACE add a single scannable anchor row under the title: [state icon] · [owner-role avatar] · [evidence pill] · [blocker pill if any] · [priority]. Evidence pill states: 'no evidence' (hollow, gray), 'evidence pending' (half), 'PROVEN' (solid check + test count, e.g. '✔ 4/4'); derive from the Evidence_Record's 4 fields. Traceability: show a compact trace chip 'REQ→TEST→OUT' that links spec id → test file → output hash. Blocker: a dedicated amber pill 'Blocked: <reason>' (reason is mandatory, drawn from a Blocked-Reason field) — blockers must be impossible to miss. On the TICKET DETAIL, replace buried prose with a fixed right rail: State, Owner (actor-role), Evidence (4 fields as labeled rows), Traceability (clickable spec/test/output), Blocker, and a timeline of state transitions. This turns the Spec-to-Evidence value prop into glanceable UI rather than paragraphs.
recommendations.where: Plane issue-card + issue-detail templates; backs onto the Evidence_Record / EARS structure visible in audit/plane-ui/screens/03-ticket-detail.png and the custom fields in docs/plane/PLANE_BLUEPRINT.md.

recommendations.title: Make state/ownership/evidence structurally first-class via Plane workflow + custom fields + labels
recommendations.detail: Human legibility starts in the data model, not the CSS. Ensure: (1) the 12 states are real Plane workflow states grouped into Linear-style buckets (Backlog / Unstarted / Started / Completed / Cancelled) so default groupings, filters and 'In Progress' rollups work; (2) custom fields exist and are required where they gate transitions — Owner-Role (enum: planner/executor/verifier/human), Evidence-Status (enum), Evidence-Test-Ref, Evidence-Output-Hash, Spec-Trace-Id, Blocked-Reason; (3) the verifier-only Done rule and HANDOFF-as-terminal-distinct-from-Done are encoded as transition guards (mirrors plane_client.py's actor-authority + the_loop.py's evaluate_stop). Consistent enums are what make BOTH the human chips and the machine JSON deterministic — free-text kills both.
recommendations.where: provision_plane.py (states/labels/custom fields via external_id) and plane_client.py (actor-authority guards); vocabulary in plane-integration/README.md and docs/plane/PLANE_BLUEPRINT.md §1.

recommendations.title: Add a machine-readability layer: data-* attributes + ARIA roles + a parallel JSON/JSON-LD projection
recommendations.detail: Make the same board deterministically parseable by agents, not just humans. (1) On every card render data-* hooks: data-issue-id, data-state (canonical enum, not display text), data-state-group, data-owner-role, data-evidence-status, data-evidence-tests='4/4', data-blocked='true|false', data-blocked-reason, data-trace-spec, data-trace-test, data-trace-output-hash, data-priority. (2) ARIA semantic contract: column = role=list + aria-label='<state> (<count>)'; card = role=listitem + aria-label summarizing state/owner/evidence; terminal states aria-disabled; status icon gets aria-label not just visual glyph (2025 'ARIA as semantic control plane for agents' guidance). (3) Emit a parallel structured projection the agents read instead of scraping pixels — a /board.json (and optional JSON-LD with schema.org/CreativeWork or a custom @context) regenerated from the Plane REST API, carrying the same canonical enums, evidence record, trace ids and transition history. This is the highest-leverage 'parseable by machines' win: agents consume the JSON; the DOM data-* attributes are the fallback/visual-debug oracle; humans get the styled board — one source of truth, three faithful projections.
recommendations.where: webhook_handler.py + the_loop.py already speak the Plane REST/webhook contract — add a board-projection emitter there; data-*/ARIA on the Plane issue-card component; consume in plane_client.py and the agent loop.

area: WCAG 2.2 accessibility + Linear/Notion light-theme restyle for a PM-SDLC Kanban board (grounded in the live Plane self-hosted board at audit/plane-ui/screens/02-kanban-board.png and its extracted tokens at audit/plane-ui/design-tokens.json)
summary: The live Plane board's current light theme fails WCAG 2.2 on multiple counts, and I have the hard numbers to prove it. Using the extracted design tokens (audit/plane-ui/design-tokens.json), I computed contrast ratios: 7 of 8 status/label text colors fail the 4.5:1 body-text threshold on white (orange #fd9038 = 2.28:1, light-blue #17bee9 = 2.19:1, green #0fc27b = 2.33:1); EVERY pastel chip fill fails the 3:1 non-text-contrast rule as a standalone graphical object (all ~1.1-1.45:1 vs white); and every same-hue label-on-pastel-chip pairing fails 4.5:1 (green label-on-chip = 1.87:1, orange = 1.86:1 — effectively invisible). Status is conveyed by a small colored dot plus a faint chip, i.e. color-alone for many users (fails 1.4.1). The board is dense but visually flat — faint borders, low type contrast — the opposite of Linear's crisp legibility. The fix is a coherent restyle: a neutral text ramp anchored at #15171a, status colors re-tuned to a single ~5.5:1 dark-tint value that doubles as both label text AND the dot/icon (every value I computed lands 5.1-6.4:1, passing both 4.5:1 text and 3:1 non-text), pastel chips that ALWAYS carry a text label or icon (never color-alone), a 2px 3:1-contrast focus ring (#3b6ef0, 4.49:1), a keyboard + "Move to" menu DnD alternative with live-region announcements (SC 2.5.7), proper list/listitem + aria-roledescription column/card semantics, prefers-reduced-motion handling, and Linear's Inter type scale (13px base, weights 510/590) at higher contrast. This delivers maximal human AND machine readability without sacrificing the dense, calm Linear/Notion feel. Note: tinted chips read ~1.1:1 vs the card surface, which is acceptable ONLY because each chip must also carry a text label or icon to satisfy 1.4.1 — the tint is decorative reinforcement, never the sole signal.
key_findings: CONTRAST — current status/label text FAILS 1.4.3 on the live board. Computed on the extracted tokens against white card (#fff): orange #fd9038 = 2.28:1, light-blue #17bee9 = 2.19:1, green #0fc27b = 2.33:1, red #ff5b59 = 3.05:1, pink #f65385 = 3.23:1, purple #9162f9 = 3.95:1. Only dark-blue #266df0 (4.64:1) and editor gray #5c5e63 (6.49:1) pass 4.5:1 body text. Against the board bg (#eff0f2) every value drops a further ~0.3-0.4. The visible result in 02-kanban-board.png: card titles and meta read as washed-out gray that disappears at a glance.

CHIP FILLS FAIL 1.4.11 (non-text 3:1) as graphical objects: every pastel background is ~1.1-1.45:1 against white (gray-bg #d6d6d8 = 1.45:1, orange-bg #ffe3cd = 1.23:1, green-bg #c3f0de = 1.25:1). A user who relies on the fill shape to perceive a status chip cannot see its boundary. This is only acceptable if the chip ALSO carries a 4.5:1 text label or a 3:1 icon — currently the labels themselves fail too.

LABEL-ON-CHIP IS UNREADABLE: same-hue text on its own pastel chip computes orange 1.86:1, light-blue 1.78:1, green 1.87:1, purple 2.92:1, red 2.29:1, pink 2.42:1 — all far below 4.5:1, several below even 3:1 large-text. The Plane editor color system pairs a saturated hue with its own pale tint, which is the single worst-performing pattern for legibility.

STATUS BY COLOR ALONE (fails 1.4.1 Level A): on the live board, workflow state is signaled primarily by a small colored dot and a faint hue. Color-blind users (8% of men) and anyone glancing quickly cannot distinguish e.g. amber 'in-progress' from green 'done' by the dot alone. There is no shape/icon/text redundancy carrying the state.

KEYBOARD DnD ALTERNATIVE (SC 2.5.7 Dragging Movements, AA) — moving a card between columns is drag-only in stock Plane. There must be a single-pointer + keyboard path: a per-card 'Move to…' menu listing destination columns, plus an Enter-to-lift / Tab-between-columns / Arrow-to-position / Enter-to-drop keyboard model (the React Aria pattern). This is a hard AA failure as-is.

SCREEN-READER SEMANTICS — the board needs explicit structure: each column = role=group (or a labelled region) with aria-roledescription='column' and an aria-label naming the status and live count ('Backlog, 12 items'); the card list = role=list; each card = role=listitem with aria-roledescription='card'. Without this, a screen reader announces an undifferentiated pile of links. A polite aria-live region must announce every move: 'AKSP-E3-25 moved from Backlog to Agent-Triaged, position 2 of 9.'

FOCUS APPEARANCE/ORDER (2.4.7 AA, 2.4.11 AA, 2.4.13 AAA) — needs a 2px-equivalent-area focus ring at >=3:1 change-of-contrast. #3b6ef0 = 4.49:1 vs white card passes comfortably. Focus order must be column-by-column, top-to-bottom (DOM order = visual order), and the sticky column header must NOT fully obscure a focused card on horizontal scroll — set scroll-padding-top equal to header height (SC 2.4.11).

TARGET SIZE (2.5.8 AA, 24x24px) — the card overflow/'⋯' menu trigger, the inline status dot if clickable, and checkboxes are visually ~16-18px on the dense board. They must present a >=24x24px hit target (padding the click area, not the glyph) or be spaced so a 24px circle on each doesn't intersect a neighbor.

REDUCED MOTION (2.3.3 AAA + universal courtesy) — card drag-snap, column collapse, and reorder transitions should collapse to instant under @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce). Vestibular-sensitive users are made ill by the cross-viewport card fly animation.

DENSITY/LEGIBILITY GAP vs LINEAR — the live board is dense (good) but flat and low-contrast (bad): 1px near-invisible card borders, gray-on-gray text, no clear column rhythm. Linear achieves calm density via Inter at 13px base with custom weight 510, 1px inset borders at real contrast, soft shadows instead of fills, and accent color rationed to a single primary action. The current board has the density without the crispness.

MACHINE READABILITY / OBSERVABILITY — for an agent-driven SDLC board, each card should expose machine-parseable hooks: data-status, data-priority, data-issue-id, data-wip-limit attributes mirroring the visual state, and the status encoded as a stable token (not just a hex), so both AT and automation read the same source of truth. This is the 'maximal machine readability' lever and is currently absent.

recommendations.title: Replace the neutral text ramp with a high-contrast 4-step scale
recommendations.detail: Anchor card titles at --text-primary #15171a (17.96:1 on white), secondary/labels at #3d4147 (10.27:1), meta/timestamps at --text-tertiary #646973 (5.51:1 — still passes 4.5:1 body), disabled at #9aa0ab (2.63:1, decorative only). This kills the washed-out gray seen in 02-kanban-board.png while staying calm/Notion-refined. Pair with Inter Variable at 13px/1.4 body, 13px weight 510 for card titles, 11px weight 510 uppercase tracked +0.03em for column headers — Linear's exact recipe.
recommendations.where: audit/plane-ui/design-tokens.json color ramp + global :root CSS vars; replaces body color oklch(0.2378) and the washed editor grays

recommendations.title: Re-tune status colors to ONE dark value per hue that serves as label text, dot, AND icon
recommendations.detail: Drop the saturated-hue + pale-tint pairing (which gives 1.8:1 labels). Use a single ~5.5:1 value per status that passes 4.5:1 as text and 3:1 as a dot/icon simultaneously: gray #5b626d (6.15:1), red #c5283d (5.61:1), amber #a35b00 (5.18:1), green #0a7d4f (5.17:1), blue #1f5fd6 (5.73:1), purple #6b3fd4 (6.39:1), teal #0d6e7a (5.95:1), pink #bd2e72 (5.53:1). All verified by computation. This single change fixes the 1.4.3 text and 1.4.11 dot failures at once.
recommendations.where: audit/plane-ui/design-tokens.json colorVars — replaces the 8 *-text editor colors (#ff5b59, #f65385, #fd9038, #0fc27b, #17bee9, #266df0, #9162f9)

recommendations.title: Make status chips carry text + icon, never color alone; use subtle tints purely as decorative reinforcement
recommendations.detail: Each status/priority chip = [12px state icon] + [text label] + optional tint. Tints: red #fdecee, amber #fbf0dd, green #e3f5ec, blue #e8f0fe, purple #f0eafb, gray #eef0f2, with same-hue label text at the dark values above giving 5.9-7.7:1 label-on-chip (all verified, comfortably > 4.5:1). The tint reads ~1.1:1 vs the card — acceptable ONLY because the label/icon carries the meaning. Distinct icon SHAPES per state (circle-dashed backlog, half-circle in-progress, check done, x-circle cancelled — Linear's set) satisfy 1.4.1 for color-blind users.
recommendations.where: Card status-chip component + status icon set; encodes the 1.4.1 'not color alone' rule structurally

recommendations.title: Add a keyboard + single-pointer DnD alternative with a 'Move to…' menu (SC 2.5.7)
recommendations.detail: Two parallel paths, no dragging required. (1) Per-card overflow menu → 'Move to' submenu listing every column (Move to Backlog / Agent-Triaged / Spec-Compliant / New-Verified). (2) Keyboard model (React Aria pattern): focus card → Enter lifts it → Tab cycles drop-target columns → ArrowUp/Down picks position → Enter drops / Esc cancels. Drop indicators get labels like 'Insert between AKSP-E3-24 and AKSP-E3-26'. This is a hard WCAG 2.2 AA requirement the stock drag-only board fails.
recommendations.where: Board interaction layer / card menu; mirror move semantics to the same API the drag handler calls

recommendations.title: Add ARIA column/card semantics + a polite live region for moves
recommendations.detail: Column wrapper: role=group, aria-roledescription='column', aria-label='Agent-Triaged, 9 items'. Card list inside: role=list. Each card: role=listitem, aria-roledescription='card', aria-label composed from id + title + status + priority. One visually-hidden <div aria-live='polite' aria-atomic='true'> announces every move: 'AKSP-E3-25 moved from Backlog to Agent-Triaged, position 2 of 9.' Inject the text ~250ms after the region exists so AT reliably reads it. Column count updates announce too. This is the core machine-readability + observability lever.
recommendations.where: Column + card markup; shared live-region utility used by both drag and 'Move to' paths

recommendations.title: Define a 2px, 3:1 focus ring and correct focus order + scroll-padding
recommendations.detail: Focus style: outline 2px solid #3b6ef0 (4.49:1 vs white card — passes 2.4.13 AAA change-of-contrast and 1.4.11), outline-offset 2px, plus a 1px inner light ring for the ring to read on dark cards too. Tab order strictly column-1-cards → column-2-cards (DOM = visual). Set scroll-padding-top: var(--column-header-h) on the scroll container so a focused card is never fully hidden behind the sticky column header (2.4.11). Never remove outline without an equivalent replacement.
recommendations.where: :focus-visible rules + board scroll container; --focus-ring and --column-header-h custom properties

recommendations.title: Guarantee 24x24px targets for card menu, checkboxes, and inline controls
recommendations.detail: Keep glyphs visually small (16px, Linear-dense) but pad the interactive area to a 24x24px minimum hit box (e.g. a 16px '⋯' icon inside a 28x28 button with transparent padding). For the inline status dot if it's a click-to-change control, give it a 24px target. Where space forbids, ensure a 24px circle on each control doesn't intersect a neighbor's. Satisfies SC 2.5.8 AA without visually bloating the dense layout.
recommendations.where: Card action buttons, checkbox, inline editable controls — padding/min-width/min-height tokens

recommendations.title: Respect prefers-reduced-motion for drag, collapse, and reorder
recommendations.detail: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { *, .kanban-card, .column { transition: none !important; animation: none !important; } } so card-drag snap, column collapse, and list reorder become instant. Keep a non-motion focus/selection cue (instant ring) so feedback is preserved without vestibular triggers. Optionally surface an in-app 'Reduce motion' toggle for users whose OS setting differs (SC 2.3.3 AAA).
recommendations.where: Global reduced-motion media block + per-animation guards in board CSS

recommendations.title: Restyle surfaces to Linear/Notion crisp-calm: real borders, soft shadow, rationed accent
recommendations.detail: Board bg #f7f8f9, card bg #ffffff, card border 1px #d4d8de with a 1px-inset feel + shadow 0 1px 2px rgba(16,18,22,.06) (presence via border+shadow, not fill — Linear's signature). Column header sticky, 11px/510 uppercase, divider 1px #e6e8eb. Radius 6px cards / 4px chips (Linear is near-square; soften slightly for Notion warmth). Accent #3b6ef0 reserved for the single primary action ('Add work item') and focus only — everything else neutral. Density: 8px card padding, 8px inter-card gap, 12px column gap, 240-300px column width. Result: dense, quiet, legible.
recommendations.where: audit/plane-ui/design-tokens.json surfaces + board/card/column components; replaces radius 0px / shadow none / faint borders

recommendations.title: Expose machine-readable state hooks on every card for AT + automation parity
recommendations.detail: Mirror every visual signal as a stable data attribute: data-issue-id, data-status (token, not hex), data-priority, data-wip-limit, data-assignee on the card, and data-column-status + data-count + data-wip-limit on the column. WIP-limit breach shows a non-color cue (count badge turns to 'over by N' text + warning icon, not just red). This gives the agent-driven SDLC loop and screen readers one shared source of truth — the maximal-machine-readability/observability lever.
recommendations.where: Card + column data-attributes; consumed by both the live-region announcer and the autonomous build loop's DOM observers

area: Live color & contrast audit — Plane PM board (list, Kanban, EARS ticket detail) vs a crisp Linear/Notion light theme
summary: The live UI is a near-monochrome gray-on-off-white sheet. Three problems dominate. (1) Surface ramp is collapsed: body is #f8f8f8 and sidebar, header, Kanban board, and every card are pure #ffffff — cards sit on a #ffffff board (1.0:1 ratio) so they have no fill separation and rely entirely on a faint hairline border, the opposite of Linear's quiet-but-legible elevation. (2) State and priority carry almost no color: the list/Kanban state pills ('Backlog', 'phase:N') render as gray text with a NEUTRAL gray dot (#4e5355) — sampled the dot, it has zero hue, so status is not color-coded at all; priority is encoded only in tiny outline glyphs, and the high-priority amber is a washed #eda16b at 2.1:1 on white (fails). This destroys at-a-glance machine/human scannability — the #1 job of a PM board. (3) Muted text is pushed too light: section labels are #80868a (3.7:1, fails AA for the body sizes they're used at) and the detail sidebar placeholders ('Add assignees', 'Add start date') are a ~#c0c4c6-class gray near 2:1 — the lowest-contrast text in the product. Real text (#1d1f20 at 15–16:1) and label-chip text (#4e5355, 7.6:1) are fine; the failure is concentrated in surfaces, status/priority semantics, and muted tiers. Label-chip color dots (#14b8a6 teal, #f97316 orange, #a855f7 purple) are raw Tailwind defaults at 2.4–3.9:1 vs the chip fill — saturated and slightly garish against an otherwise calm gray field, reading as random confetti rather than a governed accent system. Net: the board is legible for prose but muddy and undifferentiated for the structured signal (status, priority, type) a PM-SDLC board exists to surface.
key_findings: SURFACE RAMP COLLAPSED — body #f8f8f8 vs sidebar/header/card/board all #ffffff. Kanban cards are #ffffff on a #ffffff board (contrast 1.0:1): zero fill separation, cards held together only by a ~1px near-invisible hairline. There is effectively a 2-step ramp (#f8f8f8, #ffffff) where Linear/Notion use 3-4 distinct surfaces. Result: no sense of depth, columns and cards bleed together, the eye gets no grouping cues.

STATE IS NOT COLOR-CODED — sampled the 'phase:N' and 'Backlog' pill 'dots' on both list and Kanban: they are neutral gray #4e5355 (0 saturation), identical to the pill text. Every workflow state looks the same. On a board whose entire purpose is observability of where work sits (Backlog / Agent-Triaged / Spec-Compiling / Verified), the single most important visual variable is missing. Linear gives every state a distinct hued glyph; here it's monochrome.

PRIORITY NEARLY INVISIBLE & FAILS CONTRAST — priority is shown only as small outline icons (urgent = red ring, high = amber bars). The high-priority amber sampled at #eda16b = 2.1:1 on white (fails WCAG AA non-text 3:1). Urgent is a thin red outline, easily missed. There is no priority pill/fill, no left-edge accent, no row tint — priority, the field PMs triage on, is the weakest signal on the screen.

MUTED TEXT TIER TOO LIGHT — sidebar section labels 'Workspace'/'Projects' are #80868a = 3.7:1 on white (fails AA 4.5:1 for the small-caps body size used). Detail-sidebar placeholders ('Add assignees','Add start date','Add due date') are a ~#c0c4c6 gray at roughly 2:1 — the lowest-contrast text in the app and below even the 3:1 large-text floor. The row ID prefix (#676c6f, 5.0:1) and Kanban count (#676c6f / 5.3:1) are technically passing but muddy and read as 'disabled'.

LABEL CHIPS = RAW TAILWIND CONFETTI — chip dots are unmodified Tailwind-500s: teal #14b8a6, orange #f97316, blue #2563eb, purple #a855f7. Against the chip fill (#fcfcfc) they sit at 2.4-3.9:1 (decorative-only, not legible color), and against the calm gray UI they're disproportionately saturated/vivid — they read as noise, not a system. Notion-grade label color uses lower-chroma, tonal pairs (light tinted fill + matched darker text), not a bright dot on white.

ACCENT IS DULL & SINGULAR — the only true accent is the 'Add work item' button at #006399 (a dull cyan-blue, white text 6.5:1 — passes but feels heavy/desaturated, not Linear's confident indigo). There is no accent expressed anywhere else (no active-state highlight beyond a faint gray, no focus ring visible, no selected-row tint), so the interface reads flat and toneless.

OFF-WHITE BODY IS MUDDY, NOT CALM — #f8f8f8 is a flat neutral gray-white. Linear/Notion light themes use a subtly cool, slightly lifted base (Linear ~ #fbfbfb / #f7f8f9 cool) with true-white raised cards, which reads 'crisp' rather than 'washed'. Here the tiny #f8f8f8↔#ffffff delta plus pure-neutral hue makes the whole canvas feel grey and low-energy.

HAIRLINES & DIVIDERS TOO FAINT — row separators and card borders are extremely low-contrast hairlines on the #f8f8f8/#ffffff pair; combined with the absent surface ramp, scanning a dense 243-row list relies almost entirely on text alignment with no supporting structure. Crisp light themes pair a slightly cooler, marginally darker divider (~#e6e8eb / oklch ~0.92) with zebra or hover tinting for density legibility.

recommendations.title: Rebuild a 4-step cool surface ramp so cards actually lift off the board
recommendations.detail: Replace the collapsed #f8f8f8/#ffffff pair with a layered, slightly-cool ramp: app/canvas base oklch(0.985 0.002 250) ~#fbfbfc; sunken board/list background oklch(0.975 0.003 250) ~#f6f7f9; raised card/panel oklch(1 0 0) #ffffff; plus a hover/selected tint oklch(0.965 0.004 255) ~#f1f3f6. The key fix: Kanban cards must be #ffffff on a #f6f7f9 board (currently #ffffff on #ffffff) so they separate by FILL, not just a hairline. Add a one-token elevation shadow for cards (0 1px 2px rgba(16,24,40,.06), 0 1px 3px rgba(16,24,40,.04)) — the tokens file shows shadow:'none' today. Cool the base hue toward ~250 so it reads crisp like Linear rather than flat-grey.
recommendations.where: design-tokens.json — replace body.bg #f8f8f8 (oklch 0.9543) with the ramp; add --surface-canvas / --surface-sunken / --surface-raised / --surface-hover and a --shadow-card token. Apply --surface-raised to Kanban/list cards and --surface-sunken to the board/list scroll container.

recommendations.title: Make workflow STATE the strongest color signal — hued state glyphs + tonal pills
recommendations.detail: Status is currently monochrome (gray dot #4e5355 for every state). Assign each workflow state a distinct, governed hue and render it as a filled tonal pill (tinted bg + matched text), not gray text. Suggested calm-but-distinct set: Backlog = slate #64748b; Agent-Triaged = indigo #6366f1; Spec-Compiling = amber #d97706; Verified/Done = emerald #059669; Blocked = rose #e11d48. Each pill = light tint fill at ~12% + text at the 600-tone (e.g. Done: bg #ecfdf5, text #047857 ~ 7:1). The leading status icon should carry the same hue. This single change restores at-a-glance board scannability for both humans and machine/screenshot observability.
recommendations.where: Define --state-{backlog,triaged,compiling,verified,blocked}-{fg,bg} token pairs in design-tokens.json; bind them to the state-pill component and the list/Kanban state column. Map your real workflow-state IDs (Backlog/Agent-Triaged/Spec-Compiling/Verified) to these tokens.

recommendations.title: Give priority a real, contrast-passing visual treatment
recommendations.detail: Stop relying on 2.1:1 outline icons. (1) Fix the failing amber: high-priority #eda16b (2.1:1) -> #d97706 / oklch(0.66 0.16 55) (~3.3:1 non-text, ~4.6:1 if text). Urgent -> solid #dc2626 fill chip, not a thin ring. (2) Encode priority structurally: add a 3px left-edge accent bar on list rows and Kanban cards colored by priority (Urgent rose, High amber, Med blue, Low slate), so priority is readable peripherally without parsing a glyph. (3) In the detail sidebar, render 'Urgent' as a filled rose tonal pill matching the state-pill system rather than a hollow red box.
recommendations.where: design-tokens.json priority color tokens (--priority-urgent/high/medium/low); list-row and kanban-card components get a left-border-accent bound to the priority token. Raise high-priority amber from #eda16b to #d97706.

recommendations.title: Tighten the muted-text tiers to pass AA and read intentional, not disabled
recommendations.detail: Define three explicit text tiers and enforce minimums: --text-primary #1d1f20 (15:1, keep); --text-secondary ~#52565b targeting >=5.5:1 (use for IDs/counts, currently #676c6f reads muddy); --text-tertiary/muted ~#6b7177 at >=4.5:1 for section labels (currently #80868a = 3.7:1, FAILS — darken). Critically, fix the detail-sidebar placeholders ('Add assignees','Add start date','Add due date') — currently ~#c0c4c6 near 2:1 — to a minimum #767b80 (~4.6:1). No interactive text label should fall below 4.5:1; placeholders that trigger fields must not look disabled.
recommendations.where: design-tokens.json — add --text-primary/secondary/tertiary tokens; replace section-label #80868a and the placeholder gray. Apply --text-tertiary (not the lightest gray) to sidebar group headers and field placeholders.

recommendations.title: Re-tone label chips into a governed low-chroma system instead of raw Tailwind dots
recommendations.detail: Replace bright Tailwind-500 dots (#14b8a6/#f97316/#a855f7 at 2.4-3.9:1) with Notion-style tonal chips: a light tinted fill + a darker matched text/border in the SAME hue family, dropping chroma ~30-40% so they sit calm on the gray canvas. E.g. type:* -> teal fill #f0fdfa / text #0f766e; priority:* -> amber fill #fffbeb / text #b45309; gate:* -> violet fill #f5f3ff / text #6d28d9; agent:* -> indigo fill #eef2ff / text #4338ca. Keep the dot only if its hue matches the text tone. This makes labels a legible, scannable taxonomy rather than confetti, and aligns chroma with the rest of the calm theme.
recommendations.where: Map label-namespace prefixes (type:, priority:, gate:, agent:, phase:) to tonal {fill,text} token pairs in design-tokens.json; bind the chip component to them. Lower saturation of the existing editor-colors-*-text/background ramp (lines 5-20) toward these tonal pairs for consistency.

recommendations.title: Adopt a single confident accent + visible focus/selection states
recommendations.detail: Swap the dull #006399 primary for a crisper indigo/blue (e.g. #4f46e5 or oklch(0.55 0.21 275)) used consistently for the primary button, active nav item, selected row tint, and links — Linear's calm-but-confident accent. Add a real 2px focus ring (accent at ~40% with 2px offset) for keyboard/observability. Give the selected list row and active Kanban card a faint accent-tinted background (accent at ~6-8%) instead of the current near-invisible gray, so the current selection is always legible in screenshots/recordings.
recommendations.where: design-tokens.json — add --accent, --accent-hover, --accent-subtle (tint), --focus-ring tokens; apply to the primary button (replace #006399), sidebar active item, row/card selected state, and a global :focus-visible ring.

recommendations.title: Strengthen dividers and add density affordances for the 243-row list
recommendations.detail: The current hairlines are near-invisible on the #f8f8f8/#ffffff pair. Set a defined divider token oklch(0.92 0.004 250) ~#e6e8eb and apply it to row separators, card borders, and the sidebar edge. Add either subtle zebra striping (alternate rows at the --surface-hover tint) or a clear hover background so dense scanning has structural support beyond text alignment. Pair card borders (#e6e8eb) with the new card shadow so cards read as objects, not as text blocks on a sheet.
recommendations.where: design-tokens.json — add --border-divider and --border-card tokens (currently body.border is the near-black oklch(0.2378...), which is the text color, not a usable divider — that mis-token should be replaced). Apply to list-row separators, kanban-card border, sidebar/right-panel edges; add row hover/zebra in the list component.

area: Typography — scale, weight hierarchy, line-height, ID/title/meta legibility (list, Kanban, ticket)
summary: Plane's current type system is the bottleneck to both human and machine readability. It runs on a single near-flat scale (a 16px/400 body, ~13–14px regular meta, and only one real heading step) so nothing establishes hierarchy through size or weight — titles, inline EARS IDs, and prose all read at the same visual weight. The structural ID (ASCP-239) and the human title (ASCP-E8-26.4 Surface reasoning-loop alerts) sit in the same proportional font with no tabular/mono treatment, so the machine-keyed identifiers (the most important tokens for an observability-first PM board) are the least legible elements on screen. The Kanban column header outweighs its own card titles (inverted hierarchy), the ticket H1 wraps at a 70ch measure, and EARS modality labels ([ubiquitous], [event-driven], [unwanted]) — the single most semantically loaded tokens on the board — get bold body text but no color or monospace affordance. The fix is a tightened, explicitly stepped Linear-grade type scale (12/13/14/16/20/24 with a dedicated mono channel for IDs and EARS tags), a real 3-tier weight ladder (400 body / 510 title / 590 heading), tightened heading line-height and tracking, and capped measures. The real oklch light surface and Inter Variable base are good raw material; the problem is entirely scale, weight, and the absence of a monospace/ID channel — all token- and CSS-level, no structural redesign needed.
key_findings: FLAT SCALE / NO SIZE HIERARCHY: Body is 16px/400 (design-tokens.json body.size=16px, weight=400). List titles render ~13-14px, IDs ~13px, Kanban titles ~14px, ticket subheads ~15px. Effectively one body step plus one heading step — there is no consistent modular ratio, so size cannot signal importance. Linear/Notion run an explicit ladder; Plane does not.

ID LEGIBILITY IS THE WORST PROBLEM (machine readability): In the list, the ASCP-239 reference ID and the title's embedded EARS slug (ASCP-E8-26.4) both render in the proportional Inter Variable at body color/weight with NO tabular-nums and NO monospace. Proportional digits make ID columns ragged and the machine-keyed identifier — the primary join key for an agentic/observable PM board — is the least scannable token on the row. On Kanban the card ID (ASCP-243) is ~11px muted gray, near the legibility floor.

ID/TITLE COLLISION IN LIST ROWS: The title string carries the structural slug inline (ASCP-E8-26.4) in the exact same weight and color as the prose that follows it (Surface reasoning-loop alerts). Human meaning and machine key are visually undifferentiated; nothing tells the eye where the ID ends and the human title begins.

INVERTED HIERARCHY ON KANBAN: The column header (Backlog) renders ~15px at ~600 weight — heavier and larger than the card titles (~14px/400) it contains. The container label is visually louder than its content, which violates content-first hierarchy and makes the board read as headers, not work.

NO WEIGHT LADDER: Across all three surfaces only two weights are in play — 400 (everything) and a ~600 (Backlog header, EARS subheads, inline EARS labels). 600 is overloaded: it marks column headers, H2 section heads, AND inline list-item labels simultaneously, so weight no longer encodes level. There is no intermediate medium (~510) for titles, so titles fall back to 400 and disappear into meta.

EARS MODALITY TAGS UNDER-AFFORDED (machine readability): On the ticket, [ubiquitous]/[event-driven]/[unwanted]/[state-driven] are the highest-signal machine tokens (they classify each requirement's logic class). They render as bold body text only — same color as prose, no monospace, no chip/pill, no color coding. The token file has a full editor color palette (gray/peach/green/blue/purple text+bg pairs) that is going completely unused for these tags.

TICKET H1 MEASURE TOO WIDE / WEAK STEP: The detail title (ASCP-E3-5: Coverage Model — ...) is ~22px semibold but set at ~70ch with no max-width, so it wraps to two lines awkwardly and its line-height (~1.5 inherited) is too loose for a heading. H1, the EARS-requirements subhead, and Acceptance-criteria subhead are too close in size (≈22 vs ≈15) with the same weight bucket — the H1-to-H2 step is under-defined.

META ROW IS LOW-CONTRAST AND ICON-HEAVY: The list meta cluster (Backlog pill, priority/date/assignee icons, phase:1 label, overflow ...) is all ~12-13px in a single muted gray with near-identical visual weight — no tiering between status (primary state) and secondary metadata. The decorative spinner glyph beside state labels (visible on Kanban pills and ticket State) adds motion/noise without information.

BODY LINE-HEIGHT OK BUT MEASURE UNCAPPED: EARS body prose runs 16px/~1.5 which is readable, but the column has no max-measure (~70ch+), pushing past the comfortable 60-72ch ceiling and hurting return-sweep on the dense requirement paragraphs.

TOKEN FILE LACKS A TYPE SCALE ENTIRELY: design-tokens.json exposes 31 root vars — all color/editor/progress — plus a single body block (size/weight/family). There are no --font-size-*, --line-height-*, --font-weight-*, or --font-mono tokens. Type sizing is therefore hard-coded per component (hence the drift), with no single lever to tighten the scale.

recommendations.title: Introduce a tightened, explicit 6-step type scale as design tokens
recommendations.detail: Add a stepped scale to replace the flat body+heading pair. Recommended Linear-grade ramp on a ~1.2 ratio anchored at 13px UI base: --fs-2xs:11px (micro/overline), --fs-xs:12px (meta), --fs-sm:13px (UI/labels/list+card titles), --fs-base:14px (ticket body — drop from 16 to densify), --fs-lg:16px (section subheads), --fs-xl:20px (ticket H1), with optional --fs-2xl:24px for page titles. Pair each with line-height tokens: --lh-tight:1.25 (headings), --lh-snug:1.4 (titles), --lh-normal:1.55 (body prose). This single set of tokens removes per-component hard-coding and lets the whole board be retuned from one place.
recommendations.where: audit/plane-ui/design-tokens.json (add --fs-*, --lh-* root vars); consume in the app's global CSS / Tailwind theme so list rows, Kanban cards, and ticket inherit from the scale instead of literal px.

recommendations.title: Add a dedicated monospace + tabular-nums channel for all IDs
recommendations.detail: Define --font-mono:"Geist Mono","SF Mono","JetBrains Mono",ui-monospace,monospace and apply it to every issue identifier: the list ID column (ASCP-239), the Kanban card ID (ASCP-243), and the ticket breadcrumb ID. Set font-feature-settings:"tnum" 1,"cv01" 1 (or font-variant-numeric:tabular-nums) so digits align in a column and 0/O, 1/l disambiguate. Render IDs at --fs-xs in a muted token (oklch ~0.55 L) so they recede from titles but stay crisply scannable. This is the single highest-leverage change for machine readability/observability.
recommendations.where: design-tokens.json (--font-mono, --color-id-muted); apply via an .issue-id / .ticket-key class on the ID node in list rows, Kanban cards, and the ticket header.

recommendations.title: Establish a real 3-tier weight ladder and stop overloading 600
recommendations.detail: Map weights to roles explicitly: --fw-body:400 (prose, descriptions, meta), --fw-medium:510 (issue/card titles, property values), --fw-heading:590 (ticket H1 + section subheads). Bump list and Kanban card TITLES from 400 to --fw-medium so they separate from meta. Reserve --fw-heading strictly for true headings — NOT for inline EARS list-item labels (re-tag those as chips, see below) and NOT louder than card content. Inter Variable supports these intermediate axes, so the steps are available without swapping fonts.
recommendations.where: Global CSS theme — title/heading/meta utility classes consumed by list-row, kanban-card, and ticket-detail components.

recommendations.title: Tier the title vs ID vs meta on list rows and Kanban cards
recommendations.detail: List row: render the structural ID column in --font-mono/--fs-xs/muted, then the title in --fs-sm/--fw-medium/foreground. If the title string still embeds an EARS slug (ASCP-E8-26.4), set just that slug in --font-mono/muted inline so the human prose after it carries the medium weight and full contrast — this resolves the ID/title collision. Kanban card: ID in mono --fs-xs muted on its own line (already separate), title --fs-sm/--fw-medium, and DROP the column-header weight from 600 to --fw-medium --fs-sm so headers stop outshouting cards. Add a small count badge in --fs-xs muted instead of bolding the header.
recommendations.where: list-row component, kanban-card component, and kanban column-header component.

recommendations.title: Promote EARS modality labels to color-coded mono chips
recommendations.detail: Convert [ubiquitous]/[event-driven]/[unwanted]/[state-driven]/[optional] from bold inline body text into small mono uppercase chips using the EXISTING unused editor palette: e.g. ubiquitous→--editor-colors-gray (neutral baseline), event-driven→green-text/green-background, unwanted→peach-text/peach-background (it's the negative/guard case), state-driven→dark-blue, optional→purple. Render at --fs-2xs, --font-mono, letter-spacing:0.02em, padding 1px 6px, radius 4px. This makes the requirement's logic-class machine-scannable at a glance and finally uses the color tokens already defined in the file.
recommendations.where: Ticket EARS-list renderer; map labels to the --editor-colors-* text/background pairs in design-tokens.json.

recommendations.title: Tighten ticket headings: cap measure, reduce heading line-height and tracking
recommendations.detail: Set ticket content max-width:50rem (~68ch) so body prose and the H1 stop running to ~70ch+. Give H1 --fs-xl/--fw-heading/--lh-tight(1.2)/letter-spacing:-0.011em (Linear-style negative tracking on large text) so it reads as a single tight block instead of loose two-line wrap. Give EARS-requirements / Acceptance-criteria subheads --fs-lg/--fw-heading/--lh-snug, and add an overline treatment (--fs-2xs, uppercase, +0.04em tracking, muted) if you want a third clearly-distinct H3 level. This restores a visible H1>H2 step.
recommendations.where: ticket-detail content container and heading styles.

recommendations.title: Tier and de-noise the meta row
recommendations.detail: Give the primary state (Backlog) a filled/colored pill at --fs-xs/--fw-medium while demoting secondary metadata (dates, assignee, phase label, overflow) to --fs-xs in a lighter muted token with icon-only or icon+value at reduced opacity, so state reads first and metadata second. Remove the animated spinner glyph next to state labels on cards and in the ticket State value — it implies loading/motion and adds visual noise with zero informational value; replace with a static state dot in the state color.
recommendations.where: list-row meta cluster, kanban-card footer, ticket Properties-rail State value; remove/replace the spinner component.

recommendations.title: Standardize the Properties rail label/value type pair
recommendations.detail: Set rail labels (State, Assignees, Priority, Created by, ...) to --fs-sm/--fw-body in --color-text-muted and values to --fs-sm/--fw-medium in foreground, with consistent 28-32px row rhythm and 12px label-to-value gap. Currently labels and values sit at near-equal weight/size and read flat; the small weight+color delta gives the rail a calm Notion-style scan column without enlarging it.
recommendations.where: ticket-detail Properties rail (right panel) label and value styles.

recommendations.title: Add font smoothing and base UI density settings to the theme
recommendations.detail: Set -webkit-font-smoothing:antialiased; text-rendering:optimizeLegibility on the app root, and drop the global UI base from 16px to 14px (--fs-base) to hit Linear's dense-but-calm feel; reserve 16px only for long-form ticket prose if a more relaxed read is wanted there. Confirm Inter Variable is loaded with the needed weight axis range (400-590) so --fw-medium/--fw-heading don't fall back to 400/700 jumps.
recommendations.where: App root/global stylesheet and font-face/Inter Variable loading config; align with design-tokens.json body block (currently size:16px/weight:400).

area: Board spacing/density & card design (List, Kanban, Ticket detail) — Linear-grade density on a clean light theme
summary: The board reads as a generic Plane install, not a high-observability PM-SDLC surface. Three structural problems dominate. (1) Density is uneven: Kanban cards are over-padded vertically (~16px top/bottom, ~64-72px effective row pitch per card) while carrying almost no readable signal — the title truncates to a single line at "…" even in a ~320px column, so the most important field (the EARS story) is the least legible thing on the card. (2) The metadata layer is near-useless for machines AND humans: every card and every list row renders the same cluster of 5-6 monochrome ghost icons (priority / estimate / start / due / assignee / module) with no value, no label, no color — they occupy real estate but transmit nothing scannable. The one field that IS colored, the custom `phase:N` label, is buried last. (3) The global token set fights the goal: `radius: 0px` makes everything look like a spreadsheet (anti-Notion), `shadow: none` removes the card-from-board separation Linear relies on, and the board background `oklch(0.9543)` is only ~1% off the card white, so column and card boundaries are doing all the work with hairline borders alone. The list view wastes a huge horizontal gutter between title and metadata. Net: information-per-pixel is low, the visual hierarchy points at the wrong fields, and there's no calm rhythm. Fixes are mostly token + card-template changes, not rebuilds.
key_findings: KANBAN OVER-PADDING + UNDER-INFORMATION: Cards use ~16px vertical inner padding and ~12px row gaps, yielding a card pitch of roughly 64-90px, yet show only ID + a 1-line truncated title + an icon row. In a ~320px-wide column that's a poor signal-to-space ratio. Linear-grade cards are ~8-10px vertical / 12px horizontal padding with the title allowed 2 lines. Result here: ~7-8 cards per viewport column where ~11-12 should fit.

TITLE TRUNCATION KILLS THE PRIMARY SIGNAL: On Kanban, every title is clamped to ONE line ending in '…' ('ASCP-E8-29.4 Surface omission declaratio…', 'ASCP-E8-29.1 Author the subagent-output…'). For an EARS/SDLC board the story sentence IS the content; truncating it makes cards mutually indistinguishable. Column width is sufficient for 2 lines — the clamp is the problem, not the width.

MONOCHROME GHOST-ICON META ROW TRANSMITS NOTHING: Both Kanban cards and list rows render an identical strip of ~5-6 gray outline icons (no-priority slash, estimate, start-date, due-date, assignee-silhouette, module) that are empty placeholders — same shape on nearly every item. They consume the densest part of the card while being unreadable to a human at a glance and carrying no machine-legible text. This is the single biggest density-waste on the board.

HIERARCHY INVERSION ON THE ONE MEANINGFUL FIELD: The only colored, value-bearing chip on each card is the custom label `phase:N` (phase:0/1/2) — and it's placed LAST in the icon row, same size/weight as the empty placeholders. The board's actual workflow signal (which build phase) is visually deprioritized below five empty icons.

STATE PILL IS REDUNDANT INSIDE KANBAN: Each Kanban card shows a 'Backlog' state pill — but the card is already IN the Backlog column. That's a full-width chip of zero information inside the board view, pushing the useful labels down and eating the metadata row.

ZERO BORDER-RADIUS GLOBALLY (anti-Notion/Linear): tokens show body.radius=0px. Cards, pills, inputs, label chips all render hard 90-degree corners. Both Linear and Notion rely on 6-8px radii for the calm, soft, modern feel the brief asks for. Sharp corners read as 'database tool', not 'refined PM surface'.

NO ELEVATION / WEAK FIGURE-GROUND: tokens show shadow=none and board bg oklch(0.9543) vs card white — a luminance delta of only ~4.5%. Cards separate from the board on a 1px border alone. Linear uses a subtle shadow + slightly cooler board bg so cards 'lift'. Here the board reads flat and the eye has to hunt for card edges, hurting scannability and observability.

LIST VIEW: DEAD HORIZONTAL GUTTER + REPEATED PLACEHOLDERS: Row height ~47px is reasonable, but there's a very large empty span between the left-aligned title and the right-aligned metadata cluster (title ends near x~700, meta starts near x~1140 on a 1512 capture). That void wastes the densest, most scannable axis. And the same empty 6-icon set repeats on every row, so the right rail is visual noise rather than a status matrix.

COLUMN HEADER COUNT IS LOW-CONTRAST: Kanban headers ('Backlog 186', 'Agent-Triaged 10') put the count in a faint gray immediately after the name with no chip/weight differentiation. WIP/throughput is a core observability signal on an SDLC board and it's nearly invisible.

INCONSISTENT META BETWEEN VIEWS BUT NOT IN A USEFUL WAY: List and Kanban both show the same placeholder icon set, so switching views gives no new information — yet neither view surfaces the fields an SDLC reviewer needs at a glance (phase, priority, type:functional/nfr/wiring, gate). The taxonomy exists (visible as colored labels in the detail rail) but never reaches the card/row face except as the buried phase chip.

DETAIL RAIL LABEL BLOCK IS DENSE BUT UNGROUPED: The Properties rail wraps 7 label chips (type:wiring, priority:blocking, type:functional, agent:initializer, phase:0, type:nfr, gate:completion) into one ragged colored block with no grouping by namespace. It's the richest machine-readable signal in the whole UI and it's flattened into an unsorted pile — easy to lose 'which type' or 'which gate' at a glance.

recommendations.title: Tighten Kanban card padding and let the title breathe (2 lines)
recommendations.detail: Reduce card inner padding from ~16px to 10px vertical / 12px horizontal. Remove the 1-line clamp on the title and apply a 2-line clamp (-webkit-line-clamp:2) at 13px/500 with line-height 1.35. This alone raises cards-per-column from ~7-8 to ~11-12 (Linear density) AND makes the EARS sentence the dominant, distinguishable element. Target card pitch ~52-60px for a single-label card.
recommendations.where: Kanban work-item card template: card container padding + title element line-clamp/typography

recommendations.title: Replace the monochrome ghost-icon strip with value-bearing, color-coded chips
recommendations.detail: Stop rendering empty placeholder icons (no-priority slash, empty estimate/date/assignee/module). Show a meta field ONLY when it has a value, and render it as a small text+color chip, not a bare gray glyph. Priority -> colored dot+word only when set; due date -> text only when set; assignee -> avatar only when assigned. This converts a noisy, uniform strip into a sparse, scannable status line where presence = signal.
recommendations.where: Shared card/row metadata component used by both Kanban cards and list rows

recommendations.title: Promote the SDLC taxonomy (phase / type / gate) to the card face
recommendations.detail: Surface the namespaced labels that today only appear in the detail rail. On each card show, in priority order: phase:N chip first (it is the workflow axis), then type:* (functional/nfr/wiring) and gate:* if present. Keep priority as a colored dot. Drop the redundant in-column 'Backlog' state pill from Kanban cards entirely. This makes every card answer 'what phase, what type, gated?' at a glance — the core observability ask.
recommendations.where: Kanban card display-properties config + card label-render order; remove state pill in board context

recommendations.title: Set a global 6-8px border radius
recommendations.detail: Override the body radius token from 0px to 6px for chips/pills/inputs and 8px for cards/columns/menus. This is the highest-leverage single change for the 'Notion-refined / Linear-calm' feel. Apply via the root CSS custom property used for radius (currently resolving to 0) and a --radius-card / --radius-chip scale.
recommendations.where: Root theme stylesheet — radius custom property (tokens body.radius currently 0px)

recommendations.title: Add subtle elevation and cool the board background for figure-ground
recommendations.detail: Give cards a Linear-style soft shadow (e.g. 0 1px 2px oklch(0 0 0 / 0.06), 0 1px 1px oklch(0 0 0 / 0.04)) instead of shadow:none, and shift the board/column background a touch cooler/darker than card white — push board bg from oklch(0.9543 0.001 230.67) toward ~oklch(0.965 0.004 250) so the card white reads as 'lifted'. Increase the bg-to-card luminance delta to ~6-8% so edges resolve without relying on hairline borders.
recommendations.where: Theme surfaces: card box-shadow + board/column background custom properties (tokens: shadow=none, body.bg oklch(0.9543))

recommendations.title: Reclaim the list view's dead middle gutter
recommendations.detail: The list row wastes a large empty span between title and the right-aligned meta cluster. Pull a compact, color-coded meta set (phase chip, priority dot, type chip, assignee avatar) inline right after the title with a fixed gap, and let the title flex to fill remaining width with a 1-line ellipsis only as a last resort. Reduce row height target to ~40-44px. Right-align only a single high-value column (e.g. updated-at or gate status) so the eye has one clean scan column instead of a void.
recommendations.where: List view row template: column/flex layout for title vs metadata, row min-height

recommendations.title: Make column headers a real WIP/throughput readout
recommendations.detail: Render the Kanban column count as a weighted pill (e.g. 12px/600 in a soft neutral chip) rather than faint inline gray, and reserve space for a WIP-limit indicator (e.g. '10 / 15'). Add a thin colored top-rule per column keyed to the workflow stage so columns are distinguishable peripherally. This turns the header into an observability instrument, not a label.
recommendations.where: Kanban column header component: count chip styling + optional WIP-limit and stage color-rule

recommendations.title: Group the detail-rail labels by namespace
recommendations.detail: In the Properties rail, sort/group the wrapped label chips by namespace prefix (type:* together, then phase:*, gate:*, priority:*, agent:*) and add a hairline gap between groups, or split into labeled sub-rows ('Type', 'Gate', 'Phase'). This makes the richest machine-readable block instantly answerable ('which type? is it gated?') instead of an unsorted colored pile.
recommendations.where: Ticket-detail Properties panel — Labels rendering (group/sort by namespace before wrap)

recommendations.title: Define an SDLC card-meta design token set so density stays consistent across views
recommendations.detail: Introduce custom properties for the meta system: --chip-radius (6px), --chip-pad (2px 6px), --chip-font (12px/500), --card-gap (6px), --phase-color-0/1/2 keyed to your phase taxonomy, and --priority-color-{urgent,high,...}. Drive both Kanban cards and list rows from these so the two views are visually consistent and any future field added inherits the right density automatically. This codifies 'Linear density without clutter' as enforceable tokens rather than per-component guesses.
recommendations.where: Theme token layer — new chip/meta/phase/priority custom properties consumed by the shared card-meta component

recommendations.title: Tune the work-item display-properties workflow config to show signal, hide noise
recommendations.detail: In the view's display settings, turn OFF default placeholders that render even when empty (estimate, start date, sub-issue count, module) and turn ON the value-bearing custom fields (phase, type, gate). Set Kanban grouping to State (as now) but card properties to {priority, phase, type, gate, assignee-if-set}. This is a settings-level fix that complements the template changes and prevents empty icons from re-appearing.
recommendations.where: Plane view 'Display' / display-properties settings for the Work Items list and Kanban layouts

area: Ticket detail — EARS requirements list, acceptance criteria, and properties rail (03-ticket-detail.png)
summary: The ASCP-24 ticket detail carries genuinely machine-grade content — EARS-bracketed requirement clauses, four-field evidence gates, encoded labels (type:*, gate:*, phase:*, agent:*) — but renders it as undifferentiated prose body text. The EARS keyword tokens ([ubiquitous], [event-driven], [unwanted], [state-driven]) are styled with the same bold inline run as any other emphasis, so the single most scannable axis of the document (requirement modality) reads as decoration rather than structure. Acceptance criteria are a flat bullet list with no pass/fail state, no linkage back to the requirement they verify, and the encoded references buried in them (Property 2, four-field gate, nfr_subtype, check_append_only) are plain text with no code/mono treatment — invisible to both a human scanning for the contract and a parser keying on them. The properties rail is the strongest element (clear oklch surfaces, colored label chips) but is information-poor at the top (three real values: State Done, Priority Urgent, Parent ASCP-3; everything else empty) and information-dense at the bottom (seven colon-namespaced labels carrying the actual taxonomy). The label namespace (type:, priority:, gate:, phase:, agent:) is doing the job a structured properties schema should do, which means the most machine-readable metadata on the ticket is living in the least structured field. The fixes are mostly CSS-theme + content-convention levers: give EARS keywords a real chip/token style, render encoded refs in mono, add a verifies→requirement spine and a status pill to acceptance criteria, and promote the colon-namespaced labels into typed, grouped custom properties.
key_findings: EARS modality keywords carry zero visual weight beyond bold. [ubiquitous], [event-driven], [unwanted], [state-driven] are the primary scan axis of an EARS document — they classify each clause's trigger semantics — yet they render as `font-weight:600` inline body text identical to any other bolded phrase. There is no token/chip, no color, no fixed-width column. A reviewer cannot scan the left edge to answer 'how many event-driven vs unwanted-behavior requirements does this ticket impose?' which is the entire point of the EARS taxonomy.

The EARS list and acceptance criteria are two flat <ul>s with no relational spine. Acceptance criteria reference requirements by parenthetical aside ('(four-field gate, Property 2)', '(Property 1; allOf clauses)', '(check_append_only, Property 3)') instead of an explicit verifies-link. Nothing visually or structurally connects criterion N to the requirement it discharges, so coverage (every requirement has an AC; every AC traces to a requirement) is impossible to audit at a glance and impossible for a machine to assemble without NLP-parsing prose.

Encoded / machine-load-bearing tokens are rendered as prose, not code. feature_list.json, Evidence_Record, output_hash, nfr_subtype, check_append_only, in_scope, test_file, test_name, collected_at, the {functional, NFR, WIRING} enum, and the (DEFAULT true) defaults all sit in the same Inter proportional run as surrounding text. An underscore_identifier in proportional Inter is ambiguous (is 'feature_list.json' a filename or three words?), kerns badly, and gives a parser no signal. These are exactly the fields a downstream agent keys on.

Acceptance criteria have no pass/fail / verification state. Each criterion is a static bullet. There is no checkbox, no status pill (pending/passing/failing), no link to the evidence record that the EARS body itself demands ('require an attached Evidence_Record with exactly four non-empty fields'). The ticket describes a four-field evidence gate in prose but the UI gives criteria no slot to show whether that gate is currently green. Observability of the contract is zero — you cannot tell from this screen if the ticket actually passes its own ACs.

The properties rail is top-empty, bottom-dense — the real taxonomy lives in free-form labels. Top of rail: State=Done, Priority=Urgent, Parent=ASCP-3 are populated; Assignees, Start date, Due date are all empty placeholders eating vertical space. Bottom of rail: seven colon-namespaced labels (type:wiring, priority:blocking, type:functional, agent:initializer, phase:0, type:nfr, gate:completion) carry the actual machine taxonomy. Encoding structured facets as label strings means: no validation (type:wiring AND type:functional AND type:nfr coexist — contradictory or multi-valued, unclear), no grouping, no filterable typed field, and a chip wall that wraps unpredictably (the close-up shows 'typ...' clipped mid-token).

Label chips collide priority-the-property with priority-the-label. The rail shows Priority=Urgent (a first-class property, red icon) AND a label 'priority:blocking'. Two different priority concepts in two different widgets with no reconciliation — a reader cannot tell which is authoritative, and a machine sees two priority signals.

Title encodes structured identity as a single prose string. 'ASCP-E3-5: Coverage Model — feature_list.json, default unproven, four-field evidence, append-only guard' packs an epic ref (E3), a sequence (5), a component, and four design constraints into one line. The human-facing ID (ASCP-24) and the spec-facing ID (ASCP-E3-5) are both present but unrelated visually, and the four constraints in the title duplicate content that should be (and partly is) in the EARS body — drift risk with no single source of truth.

Vertical rhythm and density are not tuned for a dense PM-SDLC board. Body line-height on the EARS bullets is comfortable-prose (~1.6) with generous paragraph gaps; combined with the empty rail rows, the screen shows roughly four EARS clauses and four acceptance criteria above the fold on a 1512-wide capture. For a 'crisp, dense, calm' Linear feel this is loose — the encoded contract should fit more per viewport.

No anchor/permalink or numbering on individual requirements or criteria. EARS clauses and ACs are unnumbered bullets. You cannot cite 'requirement 3' or 'AC 2' in a comment, a commit, or an agent log, and there is no per-item anchor to deep-link. This kills traceability between the ticket, the code, and the build loop.

recommendations.title: Render EARS modality keywords as a fixed-width, color-coded token (not bold prose)
recommendations.detail: Replace the inline bold `[ubiquitous]` with a real chip rendered in a fixed-width gutter at the start of each clause. Use a monospace, uppercase, letter-spaced micro-label inside a tinted pill, one hue per EARS type, mapped to the existing editor color tokens so the palette stays consistent: ubiquitous → gray (--editor-colors-gray-text #5c5e63 on --editor-colors-gray-background #d6d6d8); event-driven → dark-blue (#266df0 on #c9dafb); state-driven → green (#0fc27b on #c3f0de); unwanted → peach/red (#ff5b59 on #ffd5d7); optional/where → purple (#9162f9 on #e3d8fd). Chip spec: font 11px/600, letter-spacing 0.04em, padding 2px 6px, radius 4px, fixed gutter width ~96px so all clause text left-aligns into a column. This turns modality into a scannable left rail and makes 'how many unwanted-behavior rules' a one-second count for humans and a trivial selector for machines.
recommendations.where: Ticket-detail EARS list item renderer — wrap the leading [keyword] token in a `.ears-modality` chip span with a per-type modifier class; add the color map to the theme stylesheet keyed off the existing --editor-colors-* tokens.

recommendations.title: Add an explicit requirement↔acceptance-criterion traceability spine with stable IDs
recommendations.detail: Number EARS clauses (R1…Rn) and acceptance criteria (AC1…ACn) and give each a stable anchor id (e.g. #r3, #ac2) for deep-linking from comments/commits/agent logs. Replace the parenthetical asides ('Property 2', 'four-field gate') with an explicit 'Verifies: R2, R4' affordance on each AC and a reciprocal 'Verified by: AC1, AC3' on each requirement. Surface a small coverage strip at the top of the Acceptance criteria section: 'N requirements · M criteria · K uncovered' so an uncovered requirement is visible without reading. This makes coverage auditable at a glance and gives the build loop a structured map instead of prose to parse.
recommendations.where: Ticket body template (EARS + Acceptance criteria sections) — adopt R#/AC# numbering and a Verifies/Verified-by field convention; render the coverage strip from those links.

recommendations.title: Render all encoded identifiers in a monospace inline-code style
recommendations.detail: Wrap machine-load-bearing tokens — feature_list.json, Evidence_Record, output_hash, nfr_subtype, check_append_only, in_scope, test_file, test_name, collected_at, schema/feature_list.schema.json — and enum/default literals ({functional, NFR, WIRING}, DEFAULT true) in inline `code`. Style: font-family ui-monospace/SF Mono, font-size 0.9em, background oklch(0.97 0.002 230) (a hair below the body bg oklch(0.9543 0.001 230.67) so it reads as a chip on the light surface), padding 1px 4px, radius 3px, color oklch(0.30 0.04 265) for a calm blue-ink. This disambiguates underscores from word breaks, stops bad kerning, and gives parsers and humans a single consistent visual class for 'this is a literal field/file/flag.'
recommendations.where: Ticket body Markdown/rich-text renderer — ensure backtick spans are emitted for these tokens (content side) and theme the `code` element (CSS side).

recommendations.title: Give acceptance criteria a verification-state pill and an evidence slot
recommendations.detail: Convert each AC bullet into a row with a leading status pill (pending = gray, passing = green #0fc27b/#c3f0de, failing = red #ff5b59/#ffd5d7, blocked = orange #fd9038/#ffe3cd) and a trailing evidence affordance that links to the Evidence_Record the EARS body mandates (test_file / test_name / output_hash / collected_at). Since the ticket itself defines a four-field evidence gate, render those four fields as a compact 4-cell strip on the relevant AC, each cell green when non-empty, so the gate's state is observable directly on the criterion. This turns the ACs from static prose into a live, machine-readable verification ledger — the core observability win for an SDLC board.
recommendations.where: Acceptance-criteria list-item component — add status-pill + evidence-strip subcomponents; back them with the ticket's evidence fields.

recommendations.title: Promote colon-namespaced labels into typed, grouped, validated custom properties
recommendations.detail: The labels type:wiring, type:functional, type:nfr, priority:blocking, agent:initializer, phase:0, gate:completion are a property schema masquerading as free text. Define real custom properties in the workflow config: Requirement Type (single-select: functional | NFR | WIRING — resolve the current three-coexisting-type contradiction by making it single-value or explicitly multi), Agent (select), Phase (number/select 0…n), Gate (select: completion | …). Render them as grouped key→value rows in the rail, not loose chips, so each facet is filterable, validatable, and sortable on the board. Keep only genuinely free-form tags as labels. Reuse the existing editor color tokens per namespace so the visual taxonomy (green=type, blue=agent, purple=gate, slate=phase) is preserved while the data becomes structured.
recommendations.where: Plane project label taxonomy → migrate type:/priority:/agent:/phase:/gate: namespaces into custom properties on the work-item type; rail renders the typed properties.

recommendations.title: Resolve the duplicate priority signal
recommendations.detail: The rail shows the first-class Priority property = Urgent and a separate label priority:blocking. Pick one authority. Recommend: keep Priority (the native property, with its icon and board sort) as the single source of urgency, and either drop priority:blocking or re-home 'blocking' as a Blocked/dependency relationship rather than a priority label. A reader and a parser should see exactly one priority value.
recommendations.where: Project label taxonomy + work-item priority field — remove/rehome the priority:* label namespace.

recommendations.title: Tighten vertical rhythm and collapse empty rail rows for a dense, calm board
recommendations.detail: Reduce EARS/AC list line-height from prose ~1.6 to ~1.45, item gap to 8px, and section gap to 16px so more of the contract fits above the fold.
