In the properties rail, collapse empty fields (Assignees, Start date, Due date currently render full placeholder rows) into a single 'Add field' affordance or a muted compact row at oklch(0.65 0.003 230) ~13px, so the populated facts (State, Priority, Parent, Labels) dominate. Keep the calm light surface (body bg oklch(0.9543 0.001 230.67), ink oklch(0.2378 0.0029 230.83)); the density gain comes from rhythm, not from darkening.
recommendations.where: Ticket-detail body list styles + properties-rail row component (empty-state handling).

recommendations.title: Split the title's structured identity from its prose and dedupe the constraints
recommendations.detail: The title 'ASCP-E3-5: Coverage Model — feature_list.json, default unproven, four-field evidence, append-only guard' overloads one string. Render the spec ID (ASCP-E3-5) and epic (E3) as a small mono breadcrumb/eyebrow above the title (next to the human ID ASCP-24), keep the human title to 'Coverage Model — feature_list.json' (the component), and demote the four constraints ('default unproven, four-field evidence, append-only guard') out of the title since they are already (and authoritatively) stated in the EARS body — eliminating the drift risk of maintaining the contract in two places. Style the eyebrow in mono 12px at oklch(0.55 0.01 265).
recommendations.where: Ticket title rendering + title-authoring convention — separate spec-ID eyebrow from human title; constraints live only in the EARS body.

recommendations.title: Add per-item anchors and a copy-ref affordance for requirements and criteria
recommendations.detail: Give every R# and AC# a hover-revealed link/permalink icon (like a heading anchor) and a 'copy reference' action that yields a citation usable in commits, comments, and agent logs (e.g. 'ASCP-24#R3'). This is the connective tissue that lets the autonomous build loop and human reviewers reference an exact clause rather than 'the third bullet' — the traceability backbone of a machine-readable SDLC board.
recommendations.where: EARS/AC list-item components — add anchor id + hover copy-ref control bound to the R#/AC# IDs.

area: Board ergonomics — 12-column agent-lifecycle Kanban scannability, headers/counts, empty states, horizontal scroll, WIP signal
summary: The Kanban board (02-kanban-board.png) is a 12-stage agent-lifecycle pipeline (Backlog -> Agent-Triaged -> Spec-Compiling -> Spec-Verified -> ...8 more) rendered in Plane's stock board, and as an at-a-glance lifecycle instrument it fails on every dimension the audit targets. At 1512px only ~3.5 of the 12 columns are visible (each column is ~330px wide), so the pipeline can never be read end-to-end without horizontal scrolling and there is no overview/minimap — fatal for an observability board whose entire value is seeing where work sits across the lifecycle. Column headers carry only a raw integer count (Backlog 186, Agent-Triaged 10, Spec-Compiling 6) with no WIP limit, no color/state coding, and no aging or stuck signal; the 186-vs-6 imbalance — the single most important fact on the board — is communicated by a number you have to read, not a signal you can see. Every card wastes a full row on a status pill that merely restates the column it already sits in (a 'Backlog' pill inside Backlog, 'Spec-Compiling' inside Spec-Compiling), while the genuinely useful metadata (estimate, date, assignee) renders as a row of empty ghost-outline icons with no values, and the lifecycle-critical data (phase:N, priority:high, sub-issue counts, label counts) is scattered across inconsistent grey chips with no hierarchy. Card titles truncate at ~38 chars so the EARS/epic identity is lost. The empty Spec-Verified column shows only a bare '+ New work item' with no empty-state copy and no count, so an empty stage reads as broken rather than as 'nothing here yet'. Net: the board is dense with chrome but starved of signal — the opposite of the Linear-crisp / Notion-calm target. Every finding below is tied to a concrete lever (plane-setting, css-theme, workflow-config, content-convention, or custom-property).
key_findings: HORIZONTAL SCROLL / 12-COLUMN SCANNABILITY: At 1512px viewport only ~3.5 of the 12 lifecycle columns render (columns are ~330px wide incl. gutter; confirmed in 02-kanban-board.png — Backlog, Agent-Triaged, Spec-Compiling fully visible, Spec-Verified header clipped at the right edge). The remaining 8 stages (the entire downstream half of the agent lifecycle) are invisible without scrolling, and there is no minimap, no column-overview strip, and no collapsed-column rail — so the pipeline can never be seen end-to-end. For a board whose purpose is lifecycle observability this is the headline defect.

COLUMN HEADERS ARE INFORMATION-POOR: Headers show only 'Name + raw integer' (Backlog 186 / Agent-Triaged 10 / Spec-Compiling 6) in flat dark text with a collapse-chevron and a '+'. No WIP limit, no limit-vs-actual ratio, no color band tying the header to the stage, no aging/SLA indicator, no count-of-blocked. The Spec-Verified header shows NO count at all when empty. The count is a number to read, not a signal to glance.

WIP SIGNAL IS ABSENT AND THE IMBALANCE IS INVISIBLE: Backlog=186 while every active stage is single-digit (Agent-Triaged 10, Spec-Compiling 6). This 30x imbalance — the most decision-relevant fact on the board — has zero visual encoding: no over-limit red header, no progress/fill bar, no heat treatment, no aging dots on stale cards. An operator cannot tell at a glance that the pipeline is back-loaded and starved downstream.

REDUNDANT STATUS PILL ON EVERY CARD: Each card devotes a full metadata row to a state pill that restates its own column — a grey 'Backlog' pill inside the Backlog column, 'Agent-Triaged' inside Agent-Triaged, 'Spec-Compiling' inside Spec-Compiling (visible on every card in kanban-leftcols / kanban-rightcols crops). This is pure redundancy: it consumes ~22px of vertical rhythm per card, lowers card density, and adds visual noise with zero information gain in board view.

GHOST-ICON METADATA WITH NO VALUES: The second card row is 4-6 monochrome outline icons (estimate, date, assignee, sub-issue, etc.) rendered as empty placeholders — no numbers, no avatars, no dates attached (kanban-leftcols.png). They read as decorative chrome, not data. Meanwhile the values that DO exist (phase:N, priority:high, sub-issue counts like '10', label counts like '6 Labels') are scattered as low-contrast grey chips with no consistent order or hierarchy — the eye cannot find priority or phase reliably.

EMPTY-STATE IS A DEAD END: The empty Spec-Verified column (kanban-faredge.png) shows only a bare '+ New work item' link on blank canvas — no count badge (header count missing), no 'No items in this stage' copy, no description of what enters this stage. An empty lifecycle stage reads as broken/misconfigured rather than as a legitimately empty queue, which is dangerous on an observability board where empty downstream stages are a meaningful signal.

TITLE TRUNCATION DESTROYS IDENTITY: Card titles clip at ~38 chars with an ellipsis (e.g. 'ASCP-E8-29.4 Surface omission declaratio...', 'ASCP-E3-22: Requirement-Amendment...', 'ASCP-E3-4: Spec-Completion Loop —...'). Because the EARS/epic identifier and the descriptive clause both live in the title, truncation frequently cuts the meaningful half, so cards are not distinguishable at a glance without opening them.

STAGE IDENTITY IS WEAK: All 12 columns are visually identical — same header weight, same white card, same grey chips — distinguished only by the column NAME text. There is no per-stage accent color, no stage glyph beyond a tiny generic status dot, and the lifecycle ORDER is not reinforced by any directional/gradient cue, so left-to-right progression through the agent lifecycle is not legible as a flow.

BOARD CHROME COMPETES WITH CONTENT: Card borders are full 1px hairline boxes with shadow on white, on a near-white board canvas (oklch ~0.954), giving low card-to-canvas separation while still adding box noise; combined with the redundant pill + ghost-icon rows, each card is tall and low-density. Linear's calm comes from flat cards, generous but consistent 8px rhythm, and 1-2 high-signal metadata tokens — the current cards invert that ratio (much chrome, little signal).

recommendations.title: Fit 6+ lifecycle columns on screen by narrowing column width and tightening gutters
recommendations.detail: Columns are ~330px today; only ~3.5 of 12 fit. Reduce board column width to ~260-280px and the inter-column gutter to 12-16px so 5-6 stages are visible at 1512px, halving the scroll distance to traverse the lifecycle. Pair with a card layout that survives the narrower width (single-line title clamp at 2 lines + one metadata row, see other items). Target token: a board-column custom property --board-col-w: 272px; --board-col-gap: 12px.
recommendations.where: Board column container CSS — Plane web app spaces/* kanban column wrapper; expose as --board-col-w / --board-col-gap design tokens

recommendations.title: Add a sticky lifecycle overview / column-minimap strip above the board
recommendations.detail: Because 12 stages cannot fit, add a thin sticky horizontal strip showing all 12 stage names + counts as compact segments (a horizontal funnel), with the currently-scrolled range highlighted and click-to-jump. This restores end-to-end lifecycle observability without requiring all columns to be visible. If Plane cannot render a native minimap, implement as a custom header band injected above .kanban board root.
recommendations.where: Custom component/CSS injected above the Kanban board root (board view header region)

recommendations.title: Encode WIP and aging directly in column headers
recommendations.detail: Upgrade each header from 'Name + count' to 'Name + count/limit + state'. Configure per-stage WIP limits in workflow settings (e.g. Spec-Compiling limit 8); render the header count as count/limit and turn the header accent red when over limit. Add a 1-2px fill bar under the header proportional to count/limit, and a small amber dot count for cards older than N days in that stage. This makes the 186-vs-6 imbalance a glanceable signal, not a number to read.
recommendations.where: Plane workflow/state settings (WIP limits per state) + column-header template CSS

recommendations.title: Give each lifecycle stage a distinct accent color and reinforce left-to-right flow
recommendations.detail: Assign each of the 12 states an accent from the existing editor-colors token ramp (e.g. Backlog gray #5c5e63, Agent-Triaged dark-blue #266df0, Spec-Compiling orange #fd9038, Spec-Verified green #0fc27b ... progressing along a cool->warm->done gradient). Apply the accent to the column-header underline/dot and a 2px left border on cards. This makes stage identity and lifecycle direction legible at a glance instead of relying on column-name text.
recommendations.where: Plane state color settings per workflow state, mapped to --editor-colors-* tokens in design-tokens.json

recommendations.title: Remove the redundant column-name status pill from cards in board view
recommendations.detail: In board view the column already encodes state, so the per-card status pill (the grey 'Backlog'/'Spec-Compiling' pill repeating the column name) is pure redundancy. Hide the state property on cards while in Kanban grouping-by-state (keep it in list view where there is no column context). This reclaims a full row per card and raises density immediately.
recommendations.where: Board Display/Properties settings — toggle 'State' card property off when grouped by state; or CSS hide of the state chip within .kanban card

recommendations.title: Replace empty ghost-icon row with 2-3 high-signal value tokens; standardize their order
recommendations.detail: Drop metadata icons that have no value attached (they render as empty outlines and read as chrome). Show at most: priority (colored, leftmost), phase:N, and one count (sub-issues or labels) in a fixed order, with priority color-coded (high = peach #ff5b59) so it is findable. Hide assignee/estimate/date glyphs when unset rather than rendering empty placeholders. This is the Linear pattern: 1-2 meaningful tokens, not a row of empty icons.
recommendations.where: Board Display 'Show extra properties' settings + card metadata-row template; enforce property ordering convention

recommendations.title: Two-line title clamp and lead with the descriptive clause, not the long ID
recommendations.detail: Allow card titles to wrap to 2 lines (line-clamp:2) instead of truncating at one line, recovering most identities. Adopt a naming convention where the human-readable clause leads and the EARS/epic ID is a small monospace prefix or a separate dim subtitle (e.g. title 'Surface omission declarations in Plane' with 'ASCP-E8-29.4' as a muted prefix) so truncation never eats the meaningful half. Title size ~13.5px/600, ID ~11px/500 muted.
recommendations.where: Card title template (line-clamp) + work-item naming convention; title/ID type ramp in board card CSS

recommendations.title: Add a real empty-state to stages with zero cards
recommendations.detail: Replace the bare '+ New work item' on empty columns (e.g. Spec-Verified) with a centered muted empty-state: a faint stage glyph, 'No items in Spec-Verified', a one-line description of what enters this stage, plus the add action. Always show the count badge (0) in the header even when empty. This distinguishes 'legitimately empty queue' from 'broken/misconfigured' on an observability board.
recommendations.where: Kanban column empty-state slot (per-column) + header count rendering for zero-count states

recommendations.title: Flatten cards and lift them off the near-white canvas for calm density
recommendations.detail: Move toward Linear-calm: drop the card drop-shadow and 1px full border to a borderless card on a faintly tinted board canvas, separating cards by a subtle surface step instead of boxes. Set card bg to pure white (oklch 1) on a board canvas at the body bg (oklch 0.954) with a 1px subtle inner hairline only on hover; radius ~8px; vertical rhythm at a strict 8px grid. Net effect: less box noise, higher card-to-canvas contrast, denser yet calmer board.
recommendations.where: Kanban card surface tokens — card bg/border/shadow/radius in board card CSS; board canvas bg token

summary: The board's machine-semantic payload (agent_role, requirement_id, evidence_status, the four-field Evidence_Record, and req->design->task traceability) is almost entirely smuggled through Plane's flat, free-text label namespace and the issue title — never modeled as first-class, parseable, observable fields. The detail panel (03) proves it: the only structured carriers are seven colon-prefixed labels (type:wiring, priority:blocking, type:functional, agent:initializer, phase:0, type:nfr, gate:completion), the requirement_id (ASCP-E3-5) lives only in the H1 title, and the spec's own mandated Evidence_Record (test_file/test_name/output_hash/collected_at, visible as prose in ASCP-24's EARS body) has NO field, comment-template, or status chip anywhere. At board/list density (01, 02) it collapses further: agent_role never renders on a card (buried inside a '4 Labels' overflow pill), evidence_status is invisible on every card/row/detail, and two mutually-exclusive type: labels (wiring + functional + nfr) coexist on one ticket because the flat namespace has no single-select constraint. For a PM-SDLC plane whose entire value proposition is human+machine observability of an autonomous build loop, the most important state — is this proven? on what evidence? by which agent? against which requirement? — is the least observable thing on screen. Fixes split across Plane data-model config (promote the colon-labels to typed custom properties / single-select dropdowns), a Linear-style light CSS theme for the new chips (oklch surfaces already in the token file, Inter Variable, dense 28px rows), an Evidence_Record comment convention with a fixed parseable template, and workflow config that maps the four agent columns to evidence-gated state transitions.
key_findings: EVIDENCE_STATUS IS INVISIBLE EVERYWHERE — the single most important machine field is absent. The EARS body of ASCP-24 (capture 03) mandates a status machine {unproven->proven, unproven->failed, failed->unproven, proven->unproven} and a 4-field Evidence_Record, yet the detail panel shows only Plane's generic State='Done'. No evidence_status chip, color, or icon appears on any card (02), list row (01), or the detail Properties panel. A human cannot tell a 'Done-but-unproven' ticket from a 'Done-and-proven' one, and a parser has nothing to read.

AGENT_ROLE IS A LABEL, NOT A FIELD, AND IT NEVER SURVIVES TO THE CARD FACE. On the detail panel (03) agent_role exists only as the blue label 'agent:initializer'. On the kanban (02) and detail-body crop, that label is swallowed by an overflow pill ('4 Labels', '+N'), so the agent that owns a ticket is unreadable at board density — even though the four columns (Backlog, Agent-Triaged, Spec-Compiling, Spec-Verified) ARE the agent pipeline. The owning agent should be the most prominent card token; instead it is the most hidden.

REQUIREMENT_ID IS UNLINKED FREE TEXT IN THE TITLE. The requirement_id 'ASCP-E3-5' appears only inside the H1 title string ('ASCP-E3-5: Coverage Model — ...') (capture 03 header), parallel to but disconnected from Plane's own sequence ID 'ASCP-24' shown just above it. Two ID schemes, no machine linkage, no dedicated field. A scraper must regex the title; a human sees two different 'numbers' for one ticket and cannot tell which is the spec ID vs the issue ID.

THE FLAT LABEL NAMESPACE PERMITS CONTRADICTORY MACHINE STATE. The detail panel (03) shows type:wiring AND type:functional AND type:nfr simultaneously on ONE ticket. Because Plane labels are an unconstrained many-to-many set, mutually-exclusive enums (the spec's own {functional, NFR, WIRING} type set) can all be true at once. Any parser keying on 'type:*' gets three conflicting answers; a human gets noise. Colon-prefixing is a convention doing a job that a single-select property should do.

THE EVIDENCE_RECORD HAS NO STRUCTURED HOME. ASCP-24's EARS text spells out the contract: 'an attached Evidence_Record with exactly four non-empty fields (test_file, test_name, output_hash, collected_at); the transition shall be rejected if any field is absent or empty.' This is the spine of the whole observability story, yet in the captured detail view there is no comment, no template, no custom field, and no validation surface for it. The contract exists in prose the agent must re-read every time, not as a fillable, parseable, gate-checkable structure.

TRACEABILITY (req->design->task, parent->child) IS SHALLOW AND UNLABELED. The only traceability widget visible is 'Parent: ASCP-3' (capture 03) and a sub-issue count '4' on the Spec-Compiling card ASCP-55 (capture 02). There is no 'covers requirement', 'verified by', or 'evidence for' relation; the parent link carries no semantic role, so a human/machine cannot tell whether ASCP-3 is the epic, the requirement, or the design node. The breadcrumb 'ASCP-3 [E3] Intent->Spec Compilation & Coverage Model' is the only hint, again as free text.

BOARD-DENSITY OBSERVABILITY IS LOSSY BY DESIGN. List rows (01) and kanban cards (02) show a single phase:N chip or priority:high chip plus a count, then collapse everything else into '4 Labels'/'10 +N'. The richest machine signals (agent_role, evidence_status, type, gate) are exactly the ones overflow-hidden. The board optimizes for Plane's defaults (priority/assignee/date icons) not for this system's domain semantics, so the at-a-glance scan answers the wrong questions.

LABEL COLORS ARE DECORATIVE, NOT ENCODING STATE. The seven chips in 03 use Plane's stock label palette (green/orange/blue/gray/teal/purple) assigned per-label with no systematic mapping to meaning — agent:initializer (blue) and type:functional (teal) and gate:completion (purple) carry color that a viewer cannot decode into a category. Color is spent on label identity instead of on the one thing it should encode at a glance: evidence_status (e.g. proven=green, unproven=amber, failed=red).

recommendations.title: Promote evidence_status to a first-class single-select custom property with a 3-state color-coded chip
recommendations.detail: Create a Plane issue property 'Evidence Status' (single-select: unproven / proven / failed) and render it as a pill on the detail Properties panel AND on every card/row. This is the #1 missing observability surface. Map to the spec's transition machine so the value is the machine-truth, not a label convention. It must be parseable from the API as a typed field, not regexed from a label string.
recommendations.where: Plane: Project Settings -> Properties (custom issue properties); render slot = detail Properties panel (capture 03) + card meta row (capture 02) + list row trailing cell (capture 01)

recommendations.title: Color-encode evidence_status with the existing oklch token palette; stop spending color on label identity
recommendations.detail: Reserve color for the one state humans scan for. unproven = amber surface (reuse --editor-colors-orange-background #ffe3cd / -text #fd9038), proven = green (--editor-colors-green-background #c3f0de / -text #0fc27b), failed = peach/red (--editor-colors-peach-background #ffd5d7 / -text #ff5b59). Keep all other chips neutral gray (--editor-colors-gray-background #d6d6d8) so evidence status is the only colored thing on a card — Linear-style calm with one high-signal accent.
recommendations.where: design-tokens.json colorVars (already light-theme oklch); apply to the new evidence chip class on cards/rows/detail

recommendations.title: Replace the colon-label convention with typed properties for agent_role, type, phase, and gate
recommendations.detail: Convert agent:* -> single-select 'Agent Role' (initializer, triager, spec-compiler, verifier...), type:* -> single-select 'Coverage Type' (functional / NFR / WIRING) so the contradictory wiring+functional+nfr trio (capture 03) becomes impossible, phase:N -> number/select 'Phase', gate:* -> multi-select 'Gate'. Single-select enforces the spec's mutual-exclusivity at the data layer and gives parsers one canonical value per field instead of N labels to disambiguate.
recommendations.where: Plane: Project Settings -> Properties; migration script over existing labels via REST API (workspace already provisioned per task #11)

recommendations.title: Surface agent_role as the leading card token; never let it fall into the label-overflow pill
recommendations.detail: On kanban cards (02) and list rows (01), pin the Agent Role property as the first meta chip with its own icon, before priority/phase, and exclude it from the generic 'N Labels' collapse. Even though the column already implies a pipeline stage, the owning agent per ticket must be readable without opening the card. Use a small monospace/uppercase agent code chip (Linear sub-label style) for dense scannability.
recommendations.where: Plane: Display -> card/list field visibility & ordering; card meta row in capture 02, trailing cell in capture 01

recommendations.title: Adopt a fixed, parseable Evidence_Record comment template and pin it
recommendations.detail: Define a canonical comment block the verifier agent always posts and pins: a fenced key:value structure with exactly the four mandated fields — test_file, test_name, output_hash, collected_at — plus evidence_kind (unit|integration). Fixed key order, fixed fence language tag (```evidence) so both the PreToolUse gate and any human reader can validate 'four non-empty fields' deterministically. This gives the Evidence_Record the structured home the EARS body (ASCP-24) demands but the UI currently lacks.
recommendations.where: Comment template stored in repo (e.g. .kiro or agent prompt); enforced by the four-field gate referenced in ASCP-24; rendered in the detail Activity/Comments stream

recommendations.title: Render requirement_id as a dedicated, linked property separate from the title and the sequence ID
recommendations.detail: Add a 'Requirement ID' text/relation property holding 'ASCP-E3-5' and show it as a chip directly under the H1, visually distinct from Plane's sequence ID 'ASCP-24'. Strip the redundant 'ASCP-E3-5:' prefix from the title so the H1 is the human-readable name and the ID is the machine handle. A parser then reads one field; a human sees one canonical spec ID, not two competing numbers.
recommendations.where: Plane custom property + title convention; detail header region (capture 03 header crop) and card sub-label

recommendations.title: Make traceability relations semantic: add 'covers requirement' / 'verified by' / 'evidence for' typed links
recommendations.detail: The bare Parent: ASCP-3 link (capture 03) carries no role. Use Plane relation types (or a 'Traces To' property) so each link states WHAT the relation is (epic-of, requirement-of, design-of, verified-by). Surface a compact traceability strip on the detail panel ('Covers: ASCP-E3-5 · Verified by: ASCP-55 · Evidence: pinned') so the req->design->task->evidence chain is observable in one glance instead of inferred from breadcrumb prose.
recommendations.where: Plane relations / a Traceability property block in the detail Properties panel (capture 03), above Labels

recommendations.title: Add an evidence-gated workflow that ties the four columns to status transitions
recommendations.detail: Bind the board columns (Backlog -> Agent-Triaged -> Spec-Compiling -> Spec-Verified, capture 02) to evidence_status so a ticket cannot enter Spec-Verified / be set Done unless evidence_status=proven and the four Evidence_Record fields validate. Mirror the spec's allowed transitions {unproven->proven, unproven->failed, failed->unproven, proven->unproven}. This turns the kanban into an observable state machine where column position and evidence status can never silently disagree.
recommendations.where: Plane: workflow/state automation (or webhook gate); column-to-state mapping seen in kanban headers (capture 02)

recommendations.title: Tune the board to a Linear-dense, calm light theme so high-signal chips read clearly
recommendations.detail: Apply 28px row height, Inter Variable at 13px/500 for IDs and 14px/400 for titles, 1px hairline rows (oklch(0.92 0.002 230) borders on the oklch(0.954 ...) body bg from the token file), 6px chip radius, and a strict two-tier meta row: line 1 = ID + title, line 2 = [Agent Role][Evidence Status][Type][Phase] with everything else in a quiet overflow. Calm neutral surface lets the single colored evidence chip dominate the scan — the Notion-refined, Linear-dense target.
recommendations.where: design-tokens.json (body bg/color/font already defined; add row/chip scale); list rows (01) + kanban cards (02)

recommendations.title: Expose a machine-readable export contract so the board is parseable without scraping pixels
recommendations.detail: Document and pin the API field map: which custom-property UUIDs back agent_role / evidence_status / requirement_id / coverage_type / phase / gate, and the Evidence_Record comment schema. The whole point of the plane is machine observability — codify the typed-field contract (added by the recommendations above) so downstream agents read the REST/GraphQL fields directly instead of regexing titles and labels, eliminating the lossy label-overflow path entirely.
recommendations.where: Repo doc (e.g. audit/ or .kiro) mapping Plane property UUIDs -> semantic fields; consumed by the autonomous loop (task #12) via REST
